title: the arrows for the date navigation for browsing past days is v small and not ...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

the arrows for the date navigation for browsing past days is v small and not noticeable. Make it medium sized

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User reports the date navigation arrows (for browsing past days) are too small and not noticeable.
- Affected component: Date navigation UI (likely in the daily tracker/feed view header)
- Current behavior: Arrows are very small and hard to notice
- Expected behavior: Arrows should be medium-sized and clearly visible
- Acceptance criteria: Date navigation arrows are visually prominent, easy to tap on mobile, and consistent with the app design system
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/tabs/LogTab.tsx
- Increased date navigation arrow fontSize from 18px to 28px
- Added fontWeight: 600 for bolder arrows
- Expanded padding from 4px 8px to 8px 12px for larger tap targets
- Added lineHeight: 1 for consistent vertical alignment
- Applied to both previous-day and next-day buttons

title: - I want dark mode as default
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

- I want dark mode as default

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `ui`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants dark mode to be the default theme when the app first loads.
- Affected component: Theme/appearance system (CSS variables, theme toggle logic)
- Current behavior: App likely defaults to light mode on first visit
- Expected behavior: App should default to dark mode for new users (first visit). Existing user preference should still be respected if already saved.
- Edge cases: Users who already have a saved theme preference should not be affected. System preference (prefers-color-scheme) could also be considered.
- Acceptance criteria: New users see dark mode on first load. Theme toggle still works. Existing user preferences are preserved.
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/App.tsx, src/lib/constants/colors.ts
- Changed darkMode initial state from false to true (src/App.tsx line 73)
- Updated init logic: dark mode is now the default; only switches to light if user explicitly saved false
- Changed C object initialization from C_LIGHT to C_DARK to prevent light-mode flash on startup
- Existing user preferences are fully preserved via the explicit false check
